user_story: As a dashboard user I see graph counts that match shipped artifacts
expected_behavior: /graph displays nodes and edges from the authoritative graph artifact stats or dashboard index
setup_preconditions: ctx-monitor running and graph artifacts present
test_command_or_steps: Compare /graph header with graph/wiki-graph-stats.json and update_repo_stats.read_graph_stats
first_test_result: Observed /graph shows 79,958 nodes and 1,778,069 edges; graph/wiki-graph-stats.json and update_repo_stats.read_graph_stats report the same counts.
notes: This answers the current dashboard count question.

user_story: As a dashboard user I see consistent graph counts on Home
expected_behavior: Home graph card uses the same graph stats source and formats numbers with thousands separators
test_command_or_steps: Compare / home graph card with DASH-NUM-001
first_test_result: PASS: live Playwright home probe found 79,958 and 1,778,069 on /, matching DASH-NUM-001 with thousands separators.

user_story: As a dashboard client I can read graph status from APIs
expected_behavior: Status and runtime APIs expose graph availability and counts consistently with page displays
test_command_or_steps: GET status and runtime APIs and compare graph count fields
first_test_result: FAIL: /api/status.json exposed local graph_store counts without also exposing the shipped graph artifact counts, which made 79,960 / 941,901 appear beside the canonical 79,958 / 1,778,069 graph count.
error_summary: Status API/page did not show authoritative shipped graph stats beside local graph-store stats.
fix_status: Fixed by adding published wiki-graph-stats counts to /api/status.json artifacts.wiki_graph_tar and rendering them as published graph counts on /status.
retest_result: PASS: /api/status.json artifacts.wiki_graph_tar.counts reports 79,958 nodes and 1,778,069 edges; /status renders published graph: 79,958 nodes, 1,778,069 edges and labels graph-store as local store.

user_story: As a dashboard user I can interact with the graph directly
expected_behavior: Hover shows useful node or edge info click highlights and focuses a node and double click returns to prior graph
test_command_or_steps: Open /graph?slug=github&type=skill and test hover click double-click
first_test_result: FAIL: live Playwright graph probe showed double-click could still allow a pending single-click drill to overwrite the intended return graph.
error_summary: Graph double-click back raced with pending node-drill load.
fix_status: Fixed by handling second-click events as back actions and cancelling stale graph loads with a load sequence guard.
retest_result: PASS: live Playwright graph probe selected a node, double-clicked, and returned focus to github; focused browser regression uses real Playwright dblclick.
